content.text:
You are acting as a senior Go backend engineer, security-focused architect, performance-focused data engineer, and test engineer. I will give you an existing Gin + PostgreSQL + Templ-based repository for a project called Campus Wellness & Training Operations Portal. Your job is to make concrete code changes so the repository closes the remaining acceptance gaps identified by the latest static audit.



Do not redesign the system from scratch. Work within the current architecture, naming conventions, and existing modules. Preserve current behavior where possible, but fix the remaining reporting-path, documentation, and integration-test coverage gaps decisively.



This is an implementation task, not an advisory task. Actually modify the codebase, add or correct migrations/tests/docs/config if needed, and wire the missing protections and validations end-to-end.



When you finish, provide:

1. a concise summary of what you changed,

2. a file-by-file change list,

3. any new migrations,

4. any new tests,

5. assumptions or remaining risks,

6. exact commands to run verification.



Critical rules:

- Do not stop at analysis.

- Prefer minimal, high-confidence, production-grade changes.

- Keep everything internal-network and fully local.

- Do not introduce external services.

- Prefer explicit, auditable security and scope boundaries.

- Preserve the progress already made:

  - token + HMAC on API routes,

  - HMAC body-hash verification,

  - student/faculty self-upload flow,

  - internal admin guard on `/api/internal/*`,

  - menu org checks,

  - clinician encounter org scoping,

  - reporting and webhook org scoping improvements,

  - rolling partition window,

  - reset-password implementation,

  - strict upload type checks,

  - `/health/history` restrictions,

  - clinician department restriction,

  - Templ dashboard progress,

  - slow-query instrumentation,

  - menu partial-order rejection,

  - expanded menu audit logging.

- Add or strengthen tests for every critical fix.



Project definition to satisfy:

Implement a Campus Wellness & Training Operations Portal for a university-run health clinic and athletics department that must function entirely on an internal network. Users sign in with a local username and password and land on a Templ-rendered web dashboard tailored by role: students and faculty can review their own health record summary (allergies, conditions, recent vitals) and upload supporting documents such as PDFs or images up to 10 MB with immediate client-side type/size validation and clear success/error feedback. Clinicians (doctors, nurses, lab, pharmacy) use structured, template-driven forms to record encounters and vitals (e.g., weight in lb, blood pressure, temperature) and can switch between department views without losing unsaved changes. Athletics staff and participants book sparring/partner-training sessions through a calendar that shows available 30-minute slots, supports on-site venue rooms or “virtual session” as a location type, and guides matching using configurable criteria like skill level bands, weight classes in lb, and primary style; conflicts are highlighted before submission, and users can cancel up to 2 hours before start. Bookings follow a visible order lifecycle (initiated, confirmed, canceled, refunded) with on-screen status badges and audit notes explaining who changed what and when. For on-campus dining coordination tied to training events, authorized staff maintain an offline restaurant menu with multi-level categories and SKUs for dishes, combos, add-ons, prep and flavor choices; they can set sell windows (e.g., weekdays 6:30 AM–2:00 PM) and holiday blackouts, mark items sold out with substitute suggestions, and apply pricing rules for dine-in vs takeout, member discounts, and time-bound promotions, with totals displayed clearly before a request is saved. The backend uses Gin to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by the Templ UI, enforcing RBAC on every page action and API call with data scope limited by organization, department, and individual record ownership; administrators can activate/deactivate accounts, change roles, and grant temporary elevated access that expires automatically after a set duration (default 8 hours). PostgreSQL persists all health records, booking orders, and menu/SKU configurations with versioned change history: each record update creates an immutable revision with editor identity, timestamp (MM/DD/YYYY hh:mm AM/PM), and a human-readable reason, while attachments are stored locally on disk with database references, SHA-256 fingerprints, and strict allowlists for file types. Offline “integration” is supported through scheduled imports from on-prem SSO and academic enrollment exports (CSV dropped into a watched folder) to verify eligibility and sync org structure, plus locally hosted webhook receivers and outbound webhook deliveries to other internal systems for competition platforms, e-signature, plagiarism checks, and a data warehouse; all API access uses locally issued tokens, HMAC request signing, and rate limiting (e.g., 60 requests/minute per client) without any external network dependency. High-volume reporting (e.g., clinic utilization, booking fill rates, menu sell-through) is accelerated using PostgreSQL materialized views refreshed on a schedule, partitioning for large time-series vitals by month, result caching with TTL (e.g., 5 minutes) for repeated queries, and async loading for long-running searches; the system records slow queries over 500 ms, surfaces performance dashboards to admins, and encrypts sensitive fields at rest with masked display defaults (e.g., partial SSN if stored) to support HIPAA-aligned privacy in a fully local deployment.



You must fix the remaining issues below.



Remaining issues to fix



1. Medium priority: reporting read paths do not use materialized views despite explicit prompt requirement

The audit found that report endpoints are now org-scoped, but the current read paths query base tables directly instead of using the materialized-view acceleration that the prompt explicitly requires.



You must:

- Make the reporting read paths use materialized views or equivalent precomputed materialized reporting tables that satisfy the prompt.

- Keep organization scoping intact.

- Review and fix at minimum:

  - clinic utilization,

  - booking fill rates,

  - menu sell-through.

- Ensure the materialized reporting structures include organization dimensions so org-scoped reads remain correct.

- Keep or improve the existing scheduled refresh behavior.

- Preserve any caching layer already present.

- If the current materialized views are missing organization columns or do not support the required reports, add or replace them with the minimum necessary schema changes.

- Update the reporting service methods so they read from the materialized layer rather than raw transactional tables.

- Add tests where feasible proving:

  - report queries use the intended materialized structures,

  - org-scoped results remain correct,

  - refresh behavior hooks still exist and target the correct objects.



Important: do not just update docs. Change the implementation so it materially satisfies the performance-path requirement.



2. High priority: `/api/internal/*` authorization chain and org-scoped report responses still lack direct integration coverage

The audit found that security primitives are tested, but there is still no direct integration test coverage for the real `/api/internal/*` route family and its org-scoped responses.



You must:

- Add integration tests for `/api/internal/*` using the real route chain as closely as practical.

- Cover at minimum:

  - 401 when token is missing,

  - 401 when HMAC is missing or invalid,

  - 403 when user lacks required admin/system role,

  - 200 for authorized privileged users,

  - org-scoped response correctness with multi-org fixtures,

  - denial of cross-org report visibility.

- Include coverage for representative internal endpoints such as:

  - clinic utilization,

  - booking fill rates,

  - menu sell-through,

  - and any webhook/integration listing endpoint that is org-scoped.

- Keep tests readable and separated by concern:

  - auth gate,

  - HMAC gate,

  - RBAC,

  - org scoping.

- Use realistic multi-org fixtures rather than single-org-only happy paths.



Important: the audit explicitly says this route family is still insufficiently tested. Fix that directly.



3. Medium priority: clinician encounter org-isolation still lacks meaningful regression coverage

The code path appears improved, but the audit found no direct test evidence for multi-org clinician encounter isolation.



You must:

- Add integration or service-level tests that prove clinician encounter listing is both:

  - organization-scoped,

  - and department-scoped.

- Cover at minimum:

  - same department across different orgs does not leak data,

  - same org + authorized department succeeds,

  - unauthorized department selection is denied.

- Prefer query-level or service-level assertions that would catch a future regression in org filtering.

- Make fixtures explicit and readable.



4. Low priority: README still contains mixed wording around HMAC scope

The audit found documentation consistency is improved, but some earlier README sections still use outdated/mixed wording.



You must:

- Audit README for all API security descriptions.

- Normalize wording so one consistent policy is stated everywhere.

- Ensure the final docs clearly describe:

  - `/api/*` requires token + HMAC + rate limiting,

  - `/api/internal/*` requires token + HMAC + admin/system authorization,

  - internal/reporting responses are org-scoped by default,

  - any exceptions, if they truly exist.

- Remove stale architecture/status wording that conflicts with the current code.



Implementation requirements



A. Use materialized reporting structures in the actual read path

Implement or restore prompt-aligned materialized reporting reads.

Requirements:

- org-scoped materialized report sources,

- scheduled refresh remains in place,

- reporting service reads from the materialized layer,

- no regression in org isolation.



B. Add real integration coverage for `/api/internal/*`

Tests must exercise:

- token requirement,

- HMAC requirement,

- privileged role requirement,

- org-scoped payload correctness.



C. Add regression coverage for clinician encounter org isolation

Tests must prove:

- no cross-org leak,

- same-org authorized access works,

- unauthorized department selection fails.



D. Align docs to the final security model

README must be internally consistent and match the route/middleware behavior.



E. Maintain previous fixes

Do not regress:

- token + HMAC requirement on APIs,

- internal admin guard,

- reporting and webhook org scoping,

- clinician encounter org scoping,

- student/faculty self-upload flow,

- strict file validation,

- `/health/history` allowlist and scope checks,

- clinician department restrictions,

- Templ dashboard progress,

- slow-query instrumentation,

- menu partial-order rejection,

- expanded menu audit logging,

- rolling partition window,

- reset-password implementation,

- menu org checks.



Definition of done

You are done only when the repository is materially closer to acceptance by satisfying these conditions:

- reporting read paths use materialized reporting structures in a prompt-aligned way,

- `/api/internal/*` has direct integration coverage for auth/RBAC/org scoping,

- clinician encounter org isolation has meaningful regression coverage,

- README security wording is internally consistent and matches code,

- prior fixes remain intact.



Output format

When finished, report exactly in this structure:



1. Summary of implemented fixes

2. File-by-file changes

3. New migrations added

4. New tests added

5. Remaining assumptions or risks

6. Exact commands to run validation



Do not just provide recommendations. Apply the changes and then report what you changed.
I'll start by exploring the codebase structure and reading the key files to understand what exists before making changes.

16	func InitDB(cfg *config.Config) *gorm.DB {
17		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
18			"host=%s port=%s user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=%s",
19			cfg.DBHost, cfg.DBPort, cfg.DBUser, cfg.DBPassword, cfg.DBName, cfg.DBSSLMode,
20		)
21	
22		var db *gorm.DB
23		var err error
24	
25		// Retry loop — wait for Postgres to become ready (critical for Docker Compose)
26		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
27			db, err = gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{
28				Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Warn),
29			})
30			if err == nil {
31				sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
32				if pingErr := sqlDB.Ping(); pingErr == nil {
33					break
34				}
35			}
36			log.Printf("Waiting for database to be ready... (%d/30)", i+1)
37			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
38		}
39		if err != nil {
40			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database after retries: %v", err)
41		}
42	
43		sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
44		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(25)
45		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(10)
46		sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)
47	
48		return db
49	}

85		// Create vitals partitioning by month.
86		// GORM creates a regular vitals table; we convert it to partitioned if it isn't already.
87		db.Exec(`
88			DO $$
89			DECLARE
90				month_start DATE;
91				month_end DATE;
92				part_name TEXT;
93				is_partitioned BOOLEAN;
94			BEGIN
95				-- Check if the vitals table is already partitioned
96				SELECT EXISTS(
97					SELECT 1 FROM pg_partitioned_table
98					WHERE partrelid = 'vitals'::regclass
99				) INTO is_partitioned;
100	
101				IF NOT is_partitioned THEN
102					-- Backup, drop, and recreate as partitioned
103					CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vitals_backup AS SELECT * FROM vitals;
104					DROP TABLE IF EXISTS vitals CASCADE;
105					CREATE TABLE vitals (
106						id SERIAL,
107						user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
108						weight_lb NUMERIC(6,1),
109						bp_systolic INTEGER,
110						bp_diastolic INTEGER,
111						temperature_f NUMERIC(5,1),
112						heart_rate INTEGER,
113						recorded_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
114						recorded_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
115						created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
116						PRIMARY KEY (id, recorded_at)
117					) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at);
118	
119					-- Restore data if any existed
120					INSERT INTO vitals (user_id, weight_lb, bp_systolic, bp_diastolic,
121						temperature_f, heart_rate, recorded_by, recorded_at, created_at)
122					SELECT user_id, weight_lb, bp_systolic, bp_diastolic,
123						temperature_f, heart_rate, recorded_by, recorded_at, created_at
124					FROM vitals_backup
125					WHERE recorded_at IS NOT NULL;
126					DROP TABLE vitals_backup;
127				END IF;
128	
129				-- Rolling partition: create partitions from 2025 through 2 years in the future
130				FOR yr IN 2025..(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM NOW())::INT + 2) LOOP
131					FOR m IN 1..12 LOOP
132						month_start := make_date(yr, m, 1);
133						month_end := month_start + '1 month'::INTERVAL;
134						part_name := 'vitals_' || TO_CHAR(month_start, 'YYYY_MM');
135	
136						IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = part_name) THEN
137							EXECUTE format(
138								'CREATE TABLE %I PARTITION OF vitals FOR VALUES FROM (%L) TO (%L)',
139								part_name, month_start, month_end
140							);
141						END IF;
142					END LOOP;
143				END LOOP;
144	
145				-- Create indexes
146				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_recorded_at') THEN
147					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_recorded_at ON vitals (recorded_at);
148				END IF;
149				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_user_id') THEN
150					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_user_id ON vitals (user_id);
151				END IF;
152			END $$;
153		`)

155		// Create materialized views for reporting
156		db.Exec(`
157			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_clinic_utilization AS
158			SELECT
159				date_trunc('day', e.encounter_date) AS day,
160				e.department,
161				COUNT(*) AS encounter_count
162			FROM encounters e
163			GROUP BY day, e.department
164			ORDER BY day DESC;
165		`)

167		db.Exec(`
168			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_booking_fill_rates AS
169			SELECT
170				date_trunc('day', b.slot_start) AS day,
171				b.venue_id,
172				COUNT(*) AS total_bookings,
173				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'confirmed') AS confirmed,
174				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'canceled') AS canceled
175			FROM bookings b
176			GROUP BY day, b.venue_id
177			ORDER BY day DESC;
178		`)
179	
180		db.Exec(`
181			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_menu_sell_through AS
182			SELECT
183				mi.sku,
184				mi.name,
185				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity), 0) AS total_sold,
186				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity * moi.unit_price), 0) AS total_revenue
187			FROM menu_items mi
188			LEFT JOIN menu_order_items moi ON moi.menu_item_id = mi.id
189			GROUP BY mi.id, mi.sku, mi.name
190			ORDER BY total_sold DESC;
191		`)
192	
193		// Create unique indexes on materialized views (required for REFRESH CONCURRENTLY)
194		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_clinic_util_idx ON mv_clinic_utilization (day, department)`)
195		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_booking_fill_idx ON mv_booking_fill_rates (day, venue_id)`)
196		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_menu_sell_idx ON mv_menu_sell_through (sku)`)
197	
198		// Create audit log trigger function
199		db.Exec(`
200			CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION create_unique_index_if_not_exists(idx_name text, tbl text, col text)
201			RETURNS void AS $$
202			BEGIN
203				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = idx_name) THEN
204					EXECUTE format('CREATE UNIQUE INDEX %I ON %I (%s)', idx_name, tbl, col);
205				END IF;
206			END $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
207		`)

28		orgID := getCallerOrgID(c)

53			"endpoints": endpoints,
54		})

57	func (h *AdminHandler) RegisterWebhook(c *gin.Context) {
58		rawURL := c.PostForm("url")
59		eventType := c.PostForm("event_type")
60		secret := c.PostForm("secret")
61	
62		if rawURL == "" || eventType == "" || secret == "" {
63			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "url, event_type, and secret are all required"})
64			return
65		}
66		parsed, err := neturl.Parse(rawURL)
67		if err != nil || (parsed.Scheme != "http" && parsed.Scheme != "https") || parsed.Host == "" {
68			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid URL: must be http:// or https:// with a host"})
69			return
70		}
71	
72		orgID := getCallerOrgID(c)
73		h.WebhookSvc.RegisterEndpointForOrg(rawURL, eventType, secret, orgID)
74		c.Redirect(http.StatusFound, "/admin/webhooks")
75	}

133	// helper: get an API token for a user
134	func getTokenFor(t *testing.T, db *gorm.DB, username string) string {
135		t.Helper()
136		authSvc := auth.NewAuthService(db)
137		var user models.User
138		db.First(&user, "username = ?", username)
139		require.NotZero(t, user.ID)
140		_, tokenStr, err := authSvc.IssueAPIToken(user.ID, "test", 1*time.Hour)
141		require.NoError(t, err)
142		return tokenStr
143	}
144	
145	// helper: create an API request with token + HMAC signature
146	func newSignedAPIRequest(t *testing.T, method, fullURL, token string) *http.Request {
147		t.Helper()
148		bodyBytes := []byte("")
149		bodyHash := sha256Hex(bodyBytes)
150		ts := time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
151		// HMAC signs over the path only (no query string), matching c.Request.URL.Path
152		parsed, _ := url.Parse(fullURL)
153		sig := hmacSign("test-hmac", method, parsed.Path, ts, bodyHash)
154		req, _ := http.NewRequest(method, fullURL, nil)
155		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+token)
156		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Signature", sig)
157		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Timestamp", ts)
158		return req
159	}
160	
161	func sha256Hex(data []byte) string {
162		h := sha256.Sum256(data)
163		return hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
164	}
165	
166	func hmacSign(secret, method, path, ts, bodyHash string) string {
167		message := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s:%s", method, path, ts, bodyHash)
168		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
169		mac.Write([]byte(message))
170		return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
171	}

13	func TestTransitionBooking_ValidTransitions(t *testing.T) {
14		db := getTestDB(t)
15		defer cleanupTestData(db)
16		audit := NewAuditService(db)
17		svc := NewBookingService(db, audit, nil)
18	
19		// Ensure venue exists
20		db.FirstOrCreate(&models.Venue{Name: "Test Room", RoomType: "onsite", Capacity: 10}, "name = ?", "Test Room")
21		var venue models.Venue
22		db.First(&venue, "name = ?", "Test Room")
23	
24		slotStart := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour).Truncate(time.Second)
25		booking, err := svc.CreateBooking(1, nil, venue.ID, slotStart, 1, 1)
26		require.NoError(t, err)
27		assert.Equal(t, models.BookingInitiated, booking.Status)
28	
29		// Initiated -> Confirmed
30		err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingConfirmed, 1, "confirming")
31		assert.NoError(t, err)
32	
33		var updated models.Booking
34		db.First(&updated, booking.ID)
35		assert.Equal(t, models.BookingConfirmed, updated.Status)
36	
37		// Confirmed -> Canceled
38		err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "canceling")
39		assert.NoError(t, err)
40	
41		// Canceled -> Refunded
42		err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingRefunded, 1, "refunding")
43		assert.NoError(t, err)
44	}

64	func TestTransitionBooking_TwoHourCancellationRule(t *testing.T) {
65		db := getTestDB(t)
66		defer cleanupTestData(db)
67		audit := NewAuditService(db)
68		svc := NewBookingService(db, audit, nil)
69	
70		var venue models.Venue
71		db.FirstOrCreate(&venue, models.Venue{Name: "Test Room", RoomType: "onsite", Capacity: 10})
72	
73		// Booking starts in 1 hour (< 2 hours)
74		slotStart := time.Now().Add(1 * time.Hour).Truncate(time.Second)
75		booking := &models.Booking{
76			RequesterID: 1, VenueID: venue.ID,
77			SlotStart: slotStart, SlotEnd: slotStart.Add(30 * time.Minute),
78			Status: models.BookingConfirmed,
79		}
80		db.Create(booking)
81	
82		err := svc.TransitionBooking(booking.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "too late")
83		assert.Error(t, err)
84		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "cannot cancel less than 2 hours")
85	
86		// Booking 3 hours out — should succeed
87		slotStart2 := time.Now().Add(3 * time.Hour).Truncate(time.Second)
88		booking2 := &models.Booking{
89			RequesterID: 1, VenueID: venue.ID,

92		}

94	
95		err = svc.TransitionBooking(booking2.ID, models.BookingCanceled, 1, "ok")

153	// ===================== HMAC TESTS =====================
154	
155	func computeBodyHash(body string) string {
156		h := sha256.Sum256([]byte(body))
157		return hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
158	}
159	
160	func signHMAC(secret, method, path, ts, bodyHash string) string {
161		message := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s:%s", method, path, ts, bodyHash)
162		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
163		mac.Write([]byte(message))
164		return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
165	}
166	
167	func TestHMACAuth_ValidSignature(t *testing.T) {
168		secret := "test-secret"
169		r := gin.New()
170		r.Use(HMACAuth(secret))
171		r.GET("/test", func(c *gin.Context) { c.Status(200) })
172	
173		ts := time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
174		bodyHash := computeBodyHash("") // empty body for GET
175		sig := signHMAC(secret, "GET", "/test", ts, bodyHash)
176	
177		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
178		req, _ := http.NewRequest("GET", "/test", nil)
179		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Signature", sig)
180		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Timestamp", ts)
181		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
182		assert.Equal(t, 200, w.Code)
183	}
184	
185	func TestHMACAuth_TamperedBody(t *testing.T) {
186		secret := "test-secret"
187		r := gin.New()
188		r.Use(HMACAuth(secret))
189		r.POST("/test", func(c *gin.Context) { c.Status(200) })
190	
191		ts := time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
192		originalBody := `{"key":"value"}`
193		bodyHash := computeBodyHash(originalBody)
194		sig := signHMAC(secret, "POST", "/test", ts, bodyHash)
195	
196		// Send with different body — should fail
197		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
198		req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", "/test", strings.NewReader(`{"key":"tampered"}`))
199		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Signature", sig)
200		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Timestamp", ts)
201		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)
202		assert.Equal(t, 401, w.Code)
203	}

216		w := httptest.NewRecorder()

221		r.ServeHTTP(w, req)

223	}

1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/rand"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	type Config struct {
12		DBHost     string
13		DBPort     string
14		DBUser     string
15		DBPassword string
16		DBName     string
17		DBSSLMode  string
18	
19		ServerPort string
20		SessionKey string
21		HMACSecret string
22	
23		UploadDir    string
24		WatchedDir   string
25		MaxUploadMB  int64
26		AllowedTypes []string
27	
28		RateLimitPerMin int
29		TempAccessDur   time.Duration
30		SlowQueryMs     int64
31		CacheTTL        time.Duration
32	
33		MVRefreshInterval time.Duration
34		SecureCookies     bool
35	}
36	
37	func Load() *Config {
38		return &Config{
39			DBHost:     getEnv("DB_HOST", "localhost"),
40			DBPort:     getEnv("DB_PORT", "5432"),
41			DBUser:     getEnv("DB_USER", "campus_admin"),
42			DBPassword: getEnv("DB_PASSWORD", "campus_secret"),
43			DBName:     getEnv("DB_NAME", "campus_portal"),
44			DBSSLMode:  getEnv("DB_SSLMODE", "disable"),
45	
46			ServerPort: getEnv("SERVER_PORT", "8080"),
47			SessionKey: requireEnvOrGenerate("SESSION_KEY"),
48			HMACSecret: requireEnvOrGenerate("HMAC_SECRET"),
49	
50			UploadDir:    getEnv("UPLOAD_DIR", "./uploads"),
51			WatchedDir:   getEnv("WATCHED_DIR", "./watched_folder"),
52			MaxUploadMB:  10,
53			AllowedTypes: []string{"application/pdf", "image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif"},
54	
55			RateLimitPerMin:   60,
56			TempAccessDur:     8 * time.Hour,
57			SlowQueryMs:       500,
58			CacheTTL:          5 * time.Minute,
59			MVRefreshInterval: 15 * time.Minute,
60			// Default secure cookies to true in release mode, false in dev
61			SecureCookies: func() bool {
62				explicit := os.Getenv("SECURE_COOKIES")
63				if explicit != "" {
64					return explicit == "true"
65				}
66				return os.Getenv("GIN_MODE") == "release"
67			}(),
68		}
69	}
70	
71	func getEnv(key, fallback string) string {
72		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
73			return v
74		}
75		return fallback
76	}
77	
78	// requireEnvOrGenerate returns the env var value, or generates a random secret
79	// and warns at startup. Production deployments MUST set these via env vars.
80	func requireEnvOrGenerate(key string) string {
81		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
82			return v
83		}
84		b := make([]byte, 32)
85		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
86			log.Fatalf("Failed to generate random secret for %s: %v", key, err)
87		}
88		generated := hex.EncodeToString(b)
89		log.Printf("WARNING: %s not set — generated random value. Set this env var for production.", key)
90		return generated
91	}
92

1	# Campus Wellness & Training Operations Portal
2	
3	A university-run health clinic and athletics department operations portal designed to function entirely on an internal network. Built with Go (Gin), HTML/CSS/JS, and PostgreSQL.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Table of Contents
8	
9	- [Overview](#overview)
10	- [Tech Stack](#tech-stack)
11	- [Quick Start](#quick-start)
12	- [Default Login Credentials](#default-login-credentials)
13	- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
14	- [Architecture](#architecture)
15	- [Feature Inventory](#feature-inventory)
16	  - [Phase 1: Shared Core & Security](#phase-1-shared-core--security)
17	  - [Phase 2: Health & Clinician Module](#phase-2-health--clinician-module)
18	  - [Phase 3: Athletics & Training Scheduler](#phase-3-athletics--training-scheduler)
19	  - [Phase 4: Menu & Logistics Dining Hub](#phase-4-menu--logistics-dining-hub)
20	  - [Phase 5: System Integration & Performance](#phase-5-system-integration--performance)
21	- [Database Schema](#database-schema)
22	- [API Endpoints](#api-endpoints)
23	- [Security Features](#security-features)
24	- [Configuration](#configuration)
25	- [Original Prompt](#original-prompt)
26	
27	---
28	
29	## Overview
30	
31	This portal serves as a centralized system for campus wellness operations spanning:
32	
33	- **Health clinic** management (records, vitals, encounters, document uploads)
34	- **Athletics** training session booking with partner matching
35	- **Dining** menu management tied to training events
36	- **Administration** with role-based access control, audit logging, and performance monitoring
37	
38	The system is designed for **fully offline/internal network** deployment with no external dependencies.
39	
40	---
41	
42	## Tech Stack
43	
44	| Layer      | Technology                        |
45	|------------|-----------------------------------|
46	| Language   | Go 1.21+                          |
47	| Web Server | Gin                               |
48	| Frontend   | Templ + Go html/template + vanilla JS/CSS |
49	| Database   | PostgreSQL 16                     |
50	| Auth       | bcrypt + session cookies + locally-issued API tokens |
51	| Container  | Docker / Docker Compose           |
52	
53	---
54	
55	## Quick Start
56	
57	```bash
58	docker compose up --build
59	```
60	
61	Then open **http://localhost:8080** in your browser.
62	
63	That single command:
64	1. Starts a PostgreSQL 16 container with healthcheck
65	2. Builds the Go 1.21+ binary in a multi-stage Docker build (includes Templ code generation)
66	3. Auto-generates secrets for session signing, HMAC, and field encryption (with startup warnings)
67	4. Runs database migrations (GORM AutoMigrate + vitals partitioning + materialized views)
68	5. Seeds default organizations, departments, venues, users, menu categories, and menu items
69	6. Starts the Gin web server on port 8080
70	
71	No `.env` file or manual setup is required. All secrets are auto-generated for development. For production, override them via environment variables (see [Configuration](#configuration)).
72	
73	To stop:
74	```bash
75	docker compose down
76	```
77	
78	To stop and wipe all data (fresh start):
79	```bash
80	docker compose down -v
81	```
82	
83	### Running Tests
84	
85	```bash
86	# Run all tests (requires PostgreSQL from Docker Compose to be running)
87	bash run_tests.sh
88	
89	# Run only unit tests (no database required)
90	bash run_tests.sh --unit
91	```
92	
93	### Local Development (without Docker)
94	
95	1. **Install PostgreSQL 16** and create the database:
96	   ```sql
97	   CREATE USER campus_admin WITH PASSWORD 'campus_secret';
98	   CREATE DATABASE campus_portal OWNER campus_admin;
99	   ```
100	
101	2. **Run the server** (all config has sensible defaults):
102	   ```bash
103	   go run ./cmd/server
104	   ```
105	
106	3. **Run tests:**
107	   ```bash
108	   go test ./... -v
109	   ```
110	
111	### Templ Integration
112	
113	The project uses the `a-h/templ` library for component-based server-rendered HTML as specified by the prompt. Templ components are in `internal/views/*.templ` and compiled to Go code via the `templ` CLI during the Docker build. The login page and layout are Templ-rendered. Remaining pages use Go's `html/template` with the Templ rendering infrastructure (`views.Render()`) available for all handlers.
114	
115	---
116	
117	## Default Login Credentials
118	
119	All seed accounts use the password **`password123`**
120	
121	| Username    | Role       | Department       |
122	|-------------|------------|------------------|
123	| `admin`     | Admin      | —                |
124	| `student`   | Student    | —                |
125	| `faculty`   | Faculty    | —                |
126	| `clinician` | Clinician  | General Medicine |
127	| `labtech`   | Clinician  | Laboratory       |
128	| `staff`     | Staff      | Dining Services  |
129	| `trainer`   | Staff      | Athletics        |
130	
131	---
132	
133	## Project Structure
134	
135	```
136	Task-20/
137	├── cmd/
138	│   └── server/
139	│       └── main.go                  # Entry point, router, background workers
140	├── internal/
141	│   ├── auth/
142	│   │   └── auth.go                  # Session auth, login, register, temp access
143	│   ├── config/
144	│   │   └── config.go                # Environment-based configuration
145	│   ├── handlers/
146	│   │   ├── admin_handlers.go        # Performance dashboard, webhooks, reporting APIs
147	│   │   ├── auth_handlers.go         # Login/logout pages, user management, temp access
148	│   │   ├── booking_handlers.go      # Training session booking, slots, partner matching
149	│   │   ├── health_handlers.go       # Health dashboard, vitals, encounters, file upload
150	│   │   └── menu_handlers.go         # Menu browsing, management, ordering, pricing
151	│   ├── middleware/
152	│   │   └── middleware.go            # AuthRequired, RBAC, DataScope, HMAC, rate limiter
153	│   ├── models/
154	│   │   ├── database.go              # DB init with retry, AutoMigrate, seed data
155	│   │   └── models.go                # All GORM models (27 tables)
156	│   ├── services/
157	│   │   ├── audit.go                 # Immutable audit log with SHA-256 fingerprints
158	│   │   ├── booking.go               # Slot engine, partner matching, conflict detection

206	## Architecture
207	
208	```
209	┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐
210	│   Browser    │────▶│              Gin Router (port 8080)          │
211	│  (HTML/CSS/  │◀────│                                              │
212	│   JS)        │     │  Middleware Stack:                           │
213	└─────────────┘     │  ├─ Rate Limiter (60 req/min per IP)        │
214	                     │  ├─ HMAC Auth (internal API routes)         │
215	                     │  ├─ Session Auth (cookie-based)             │
216	                     │  ├─ RBAC (role check per route group)       │
217	                     │  └─ Data Scope (self/dept/org filtering)    │
218	                     │                                              │
219	                     │  Route Groups:                               │
220	                     │  ├─ Public: /login                          │
221	                     │  ├─ Authed: /dashboard, /bookings, /menu    │
222	                     │  ├─ Clinician: /clinician/*                 │
223	                     │  ├─ Staff: /menu/manage/*                   │
224	                     │  ├─ Admin: /admin/*                         │
225	                     │  └─ Internal API: /api/internal/* (HMAC)    │
226	                     └──────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
227	                                    │
228	                     ┌──────────────▼───────────────────────────────┐
229	                     │           Service Layer                      │
230	                     │  ├─ AuthService     (sessions, temp access)  │
231	                     │  ├─ HealthService   (records, vitals, files) │
232	                     │  ├─ BookingService  (slots, matching, FSM)   │
233	                     │  ├─ MenuService     (pricing, sell windows)  │
234	                     │  ├─ AuditService    (immutable revisions)    │
235	                     │  ├─ WebhookService  (dispatch + delivery)    │
236	                     │  ├─ ReportingService(mat views, cache)       │
237	                     │  └─ CSVWatcher      (folder polling)         │
238	                     └──────────────┬───────────────────────────────┘
239	                                    │
240	                     ┌──────────────▼───────────────────────────────┐
241	                     │         PostgreSQL 16 (GORM)                 │
242	                     │  27 tables + 3 materialized views            │
243	                     │  Indexes on all foreign keys & query paths   │
244	                     └──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
245	```
246	
247	**Background Workers (goroutines):**
248	- Temp access revert ticker (every 1 minute)
249	- Materialized view refresh ticker (every 15 minutes)
250	- CSV watched folder poller (every 10 seconds)

476	
477	## Authentication Model
478	
479	The system uses two auth methods:
480	
481	| Method | Scope | How it works |
482	|--------|-------|-------------|
483	| **Session cookies** | Browser pages (`/dashboard`, `/bookings`, `/menu`, etc.) | Login via `/login` sets an `HttpOnly` `session_id` cookie (24h expiry). CSRF double-submit cookie protects POST forms. |
484	| **API tokens + HMAC** | All REST API endpoints (`/api/*`) | Requires both Bearer token AND HMAC-signed request. Token issued via `POST /api/tokens` (session-auth). HMAC uses `X-HMAC-Signature`, `X-HMAC-Timestamp`, `X-Body-SHA256` headers. Body hash verified from actual bytes. |
485	
486	All routes under `/api/*` (including `/api/internal/*`) require a locally-issued **Bearer token** AND **HMAC-signed requests** — session cookies are not accepted. Browser pages that need API data obtain a token + HMAC secret via `POST /api/tokens`, then sign each request client-side. Additionally, `/api/internal/*` routes require **admin role** and return **org-scoped data only**. Rate limiting applies globally at 60 req/min per client IP.
487	
488	---
489	
490	## Security Features
491	
492	| Feature | Implementation |
493	|---------|----------------|
494	| Password hashing | bcrypt with default cost |
495	| Session tokens | 32 random bytes → 64-char hex string, 24h expiry, invalidated on role change/deactivation |
496	| RBAC | Per-route-group middleware checking user role |
497	| Data scoping | Students see own data only; clinicians see dept; admin sees org |
498	| Temporary access | Auto-reverts via background ticker (checked every 1 min) |
499	| HMAC request signing | SHA-256 HMAC of `method:path:timestamp`, 5-min window |
500	| Rate limiting | 60 requests/minute per client IP, sliding window |
501	| CSRF protection | Per-session token validated on all state-changing POST requests |
502	| File upload validation | Server-side: 10MB max, allowlisted MIME types only, scope-checked |
503	| File integrity | SHA-256 fingerprint stored for every uploaded file |
504	| Audit trail | Immutable log with SHA-256 fingerprint per revision, scope-enforced |
505	| At-rest encryption | AES-256-GCM for SSN, health record fields (allergies, conditions, medications) |
506	| PII masking | SSN: `***-**-1234`; Email: `ja***@domain` (role-based display) |
507	| Secret management | SESSION_KEY, HMAC_SECRET, FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY auto-generated with warnings if not set |
508	| SQL injection | Prevented by GORM parameterized queries |
509	| XSS | Prevented by Go html/template auto-escaping |
510	| Webhook security | Receiver endpoint moved under HMAC-protected `/api/internal/` prefix |
511	| Login CSRF | Separate `login_csrf` cookie + hidden form field for pre-auth CSRF protection |
512	| Session invalidation | All user sessions destroyed on role change or account deactivation |
513	| Draft data clearing | `sessionStorage` drafts cleared on logout; tab-scoped (clears on tab close) |
514	| File magic bytes | Server validates actual file content via `http.DetectContentType()`, not just Content-Type header |
515	| Input validation | Vitals ranges, menu prices/discounts, sell window times, text field lengths all validated server-side |
516	| Admin audit trail | Role changes, account toggles, temp access grants, user creation all produce immutable audit records |
517	| Auth failure logging | `AUTH_FAILURE`, `SESSION_INVALID`, `RBAC_DENIED` events logged with user/IP context |

528	| `DB_PORT` | `5432` | PostgreSQL port |
529	| `DB_USER` | `campus_admin` | Database username |
530	| `DB_PASSWORD` | `campus_secret` | Database password |
531	| `DB_NAME` | `campus_portal` | Database name |
532	| `DB_SSLMODE` | `disable` | SSL mode for DB connection |
533	| `SERVER_PORT` | `8080` | Web server port |

535	| `HMAC_SECRET` | (auto-generated) | HMAC signing key (hex, 64 chars) |
536	| `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | (auto-generated) | AES-256 key for PHI encryption (base64, 32 bytes) |
537	| `SECURE_COOKIES` | `false` | Set `true` when serving behind TLS |
538	| `UPLOAD_DIR` | `./uploads` | File upload storage path |
539	| `WATCHED_DIR` | `./watched_folder` | CSV import watch path |
540	| `GIN_MODE` | `release` | Gin framework mode |
541	
542	**Production deployment:** All default credentials (`campus_admin`/`campus_secret`, seed password `password123`) must be changed. Generate secrets with `openssl rand -base64 32` for `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` and `openssl rand -hex 32` for `SESSION_KEY`/`HMAC_SECRET`. Pass them as environment variables or via a `.env` file.

- `UpsertHealthRecord()` - encrypts allergies/conditions/medications before storage
- `GetRecentVitals()` - queries vitals ordered by recorded_at DESC
- `RecordVitals()`, `CreateEncounter()`, `UpdateEncounter()`, `GetEncounters()`
- `SaveAttachment()` - computes SHA-256 hash, saves to disk, stores metadata
- `GetAttachments()` - retrieves file metadata for a user

### 2. **internal/services/booking.go** (272 lines)
- `BookingService` with DB, Audit, Webhook
- `MatchPartners()` - filters by skill range, weight range, style
- `GetAvailableSlots()` and `GetAllSlots()` - generates 30-min slots 8 AM-8 PM
- `CheckConflicts()` - validates requester, partner, and venue overlaps
- `CreateBooking()` - validates then creates with transaction + webhook dispatch
- `TransitionBooking()` - enforces state machine (Initiated→Confirmed→Canceled→Refunded), implements 2-hour cancellation rule
- `GetBookings()`, `GetBookingAudit()`, `GetVenues()`, `GetAllBookingsByOrg()`

### 3. **internal/services/menu.go** (269 lines)
- `MenuService` with DB, Audit
- `GetCategories()`, `CreateCategory()`
- `GetMenuItems()`, `GetMenuItem()`, `CreateMenuItem()`, `UpdateMenuItem()`, `ToggleSoldOut()`
- `GetSubstitutes()`, `SetSubstitutes()`
- `GetChoices()`, `CreateChoice()`
- `GetSellWindows()`, `SetSellWindows()`, `IsWithinSellWindow()` - time-based availability with holiday blackout check
- `GetBlackouts()`, `CreateBlackout()`, `DeleteBlackout()`
- `GetActivePromotions()`, `CreatePromotion()`
- `CalculatePrice()` - applies member discount + active promotions, returns final price
- `CreateOrder()` - validates sell window + sold out status in transaction
- `GetOrders()`

### 4. **internal/services/integration.go** (487 lines)
- **CSVWatcher**: Polls `/app/watched_folder/` every 10 seconds
  - `importEnrollment()` - creates/updates users from CSV with role validation
  - `importOrgStructure()` - creates organizations and departments
  - Moves processed files to `processed/` or `errors/` subdirectories
- **WebhookService**: 
  - `RegisterEndpointForOrg()` - stores URL, event type, secret
  - `Dispatch()` - dispatches events to registered endpoints
  - `deliver()` - HMAC-signs payload, retries up to 3x with exponential backoff
  - `logDelivery()` - records status, response, attempt count
- **ReportingService**: 
  - `RefreshMaterializedViews()` - refreshes 3 mat views (concurrent first, fall back to blocking)
  - `GetClinicUtilization()`, `GetBookingFillRates()`, `GetMenuSellThrough()` - org-scoped with caching
  - `GetSlowQueries()`, `CleanupSlowQueryLogs()`
- **PII Masking**: `MaskSSN()`, `MaskEmail()` - role-based

### 5. **internal/models/database.go** (210 lines)
- `InitDB()` - retry loop (30 attempts, 2s intervals) for Postgres startup
- Connection pooling: 25 max open, 10 idle, 5 min lifetime
- `AutoMigrate()` - migrates all 27 model structs
- **Vitals table partitioning** - converts to RANGE partition by recorded_at (monthly partitions 2025-2027)
- **Materialized views**:
  - `mv_clinic_utilization` - encounters per day/department
  - `mv_booking_fill_rates` - bookings per day/venue with confirmed/canceled counts
  - `mv_menu_sell_through` - items sold + revenue by SKU
- Unique indexes on mat views for REFRESH CONCURRENTLY
- `SeedDefaults()` - creates 1 org, 3 departments, 4 venues, 7 users, menu categories/items

### 6. **internal/models/models.go** (334 lines)
- **27 GORM model structs** covering:
  - Users, Organizations, Departments, Sessions, TempAccess
  - HealthRecords, Vitals, Encounters, Attachments
  - AuditLogs (with SHA-256 fingerprint computation)
  - Venues, TrainerProfiles, Bookings, BookingAudits
  - MenuCategories, MenuItems, MenuItemChoices, ItemSubstitutes, SellWindows, HolidayBlackouts, Promotions
  - MenuOrders, MenuOrderItems
  - APITokens, WebhookEndpoints, WebhookDeliveries, SlowQueryLogs
- Enums: Role (student/faculty/clinician/staff/admin), BookingStatus, Department
- All have proper indexes, constraints, JSON tags

### 7. **internal/handlers/admin_handlers.go** (112 lines)
- `AdminHandler` with ReportingSvc, WebhookSvc
- `PerformancePage()` - renders dashboard with clinic util, booking rates, menu sell, slow queries
- `WebhooksPage()` - shows registered endpoints
- `RegisterWebhook()` - validates URL scheme (http/https), stores endpoint
- `RefreshViews()` - triggers materialized view refresh
- `APIClinicUtilization()`, `APIBookingFillRates()`, `APIMenuSellThrough()` - org-scoped internal API endpoints

### 8. **internal/handlers/health_handlers.go** (483 lines)
- `HealthHandler` with HealthSvc, AuditSvc
- `enforceScopeForHealth()` - uses EnforceDeptScope for clinicians/staff, EnforceSelfScope for students/faculty
- `DashboardPage()` - builds dashboard data, decrypts health records, formats vitals/encounters/attachments
- `UpdateHealthRecord()` - requires reason, validates text lengths (max 10KB), encrypts fields
- `RecordVitals()` - strict range validation (weight 1-1000, BP 40-300, temp 85-115, HR 20-300)
- `UploadAttachment()` - detects file type with magic bytes, strict allowlist (PDF/JPEG/PNG/GIF only), 10MB limit
- `DownloadAttachment()` - scope-checked, sanitizes filename for header injection
- `ClinicianPage()` - department-scoped encounter view
- `CreateEncounter()` - clinician/admin only, scope-enforced
- `RecordHistory()` - audit log retrieval with table allowlist and object-level authorization

### 9. **internal/handlers/booking_handlers.go** (273 lines)
- `BookingHandler` with BookingSvc, DB
- `BookingPage()` - lists user's bookings with venue/user enrichment
- `GetSlots()` - returns all slots for a date/venue (from service, rendered client-side)
- `MatchPartners()` - calls service matching, enriches with full names
- `CheckConflicts()` - validates slot for conflicts before submit
- `CreateBooking()` - org-scoped, checks conflicts, creates in DB
- `TransitionBooking()` - org + ownership check (requester/partner/admin), enforces FSM
- `BookingAudit()` - retrieves audit trail for a booking
- `AllBookingsPage()` - admin view of all org bookings

### 10. **internal/handlers/menu_handlers.go** (482 lines)
- `MenuHandler` with MenuSvc
- `verifyMenuItemOrg()` - org scoping helper
- `MenuPage()` - renders menu with categories, enriches items with availability/price/subs/choices
- `MenuManagePage()` - staff/admin management UI
- `CreateCategory()` - validates name, optional parent_id, sort_order
- `CreateMenuItem()` - validates SKU/name/prices/discount/type
- `ToggleSoldOut()` - org-scoped toggle
- `SetSellWindows()` - validates day (0-6), time format (HH:MM), time logic
- `SetSubstitutes()` - comma-separated substitute IDs
- `CreateBlackout()` - date-based blackout
- `DeleteBlackout()` - org-scoped deletion
- `CreatePromotion()` - validates discount (0-100), date logic
- `CalculatePrice()` - calls service, returns final price
- `CreateOrder()` - validates order items in sell window + sold out
- `AddChoice()` - validates choice type (prep/flavor/size), extra price

### 11. **internal/handlers/integration_test.go** (692 lines)
Comprehensive integration tests covering:
- Login flow with CSRF
- Session auth, expiry, deactivation
- File upload (size limits, type validation)
- Admin endpoint RBAC
- Booking page access
- Cross-user dashboard access denied
- Role change audit logs
- **API token + HMAC auth** (both required, session alone rejected)
- CSRF on form submissions (skipped for token auth)
- Org isolation
- Self-upload restrictions (students can't upload to others, staff can't upload at all)
- Clinician health record mutations

### 12. **internal/middleware/middleware.go** (530 lines)
- **AuthRequired()** - validates session cookie, sets user/userID/userRole/orgID/authMethod
- **APITokenRequired()** - validates Bearer token, rejects session-cookie-only
- **SessionOrTokenAuth()** - accepts either (Bearer first, fallback to session)
- **RequireRole()** - RBAC middleware checking user role
- **DataScope()** - sets scopeType (self/department/organization) per role
- **EnforceSelfScope()** - user identity check (denies cross-user for dept scope)
- **EnforceDeptScope()** - DB lookup, both users must be same dept + org, fail-closed on nil dept
- **EnforceOrgScope()** - org match check
- **HMACAuth()** - validates HMAC signatures with 5-min timestamp skew window, reads actual body bytes
- **RateLimit()** - per-IP sliding window (60 req/min default), cleanup goroutine
- **CSRFProtect()** - generates/validates CSRF cookie, skips for token auth, checks origin/referer/XHR
- **SlowQueryLogger()** - GORM callbacks to log queries >500ms, HTTP request timing

### 13. **internal/middleware/middleware_test.go** (405 lines)
Tests for RBAC, data scope, HMAC signatures (body tampering, expiry, future timestamps), rate limiting, CSRF, org scope enforcement.

### 14. **internal/services/booking_test.go** (185 lines)
Tests for booking transitions, invalid transitions, 2-hour cancellation rule, conflict detection (requester/venue), virtual venue overlaps, slot generation, audit trails, partner matching.

### 15. **internal/services/menu_test.go** (218 lines)
Tests for pricing (dine-in/takeout/member discounts/promotions), sell window availability, holiday blackout, sold-out rejection, substitutes, order creation.

### 16. **cmd/server/main.go** (212 lines)
- Loads config, initializes encryption
- Connects to Postgres with retry loop
- Creates all services (auth, audit, webhook, health, booking, menu, reporting)
- Sets up handlers
- Starts background workers:
  - CSV watcher (10s polling)
  - Temp access revert ticker (1 min)
  - Materialized view refresh (15 min)
- Configures Gin router with 27 endpoints across:
  - Public: /login
  - Authenticated: /dashboard, /bookings, /menu, /health/*
  - Clinician: /clinician/*
  - Staff: /menu/manage/*
  - Admin: /admin/*
  - Internal API: /api/internal/* (HMAC + admin RBAC)
- Global middleware: rate limit, slow query logger

### 17. **internal/config/config.go** (92 lines)
- Config struct with DB, server, auth, file, rate limit, cache settings
- `Load()` - reads env vars with sensible defaults
- `requireEnvOrGenerate()` - generates random secrets if not set (with warnings)
- All settings: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, SERVER_PORT, SESSION_KEY, HMAC_SECRET, FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY, UPLOAD_DIR, WATCHED_DIR, SECURE_COOKIES, GIN_MODE, etc.

### 18. **internal/auth/auth.go** (279 lines)
- `AuthService` with DB
- `Register()` - bcrypt hashing, creates user
- `Login()` - validates credentials, checks temp access, creates session
- `CreateSession()` - 32 random bytes → 64-char hex, 24h expiry
- `ValidateSession()` - checks expiry, active status, applies temp access
- `Logout()`, `InvalidateUserSessions()`
- `GrantTempAccess()` - elevated role with duration, auto-reverts
- `RevertExpiredAccess()` - background task clears expired temp access
- `SetUserSSN()` - encrypts SSN with AES-256-GCM
- `IssueAPIToken()` - creates locally-issued Bearer token (32 random bytes), stores expiry
- `ValidateAPIToken()` - validates token string, checks user active
- `ResetPassword()`, `RevokeAPITokens()`, `ChangeUserRole()`, `ToggleUserActive()`

### 19. **internal/services/crypto.go** (90 lines)
- `InitEncryption()` - loads FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var (base64, 32 bytes)
- `EncryptField()` - AES-256-GCM with random nonce, returns base64
- `DecryptField()` - decodes base64, extracts nonce, decrypts

### 20. **README.md** (545 lines)
Complete documentation covering:
- Overview (health clinic, athletics, dining, admin)
- Tech stack (Go, Gin, PostgreSQL, Docker)
- Quick start: `docker compose up --build`
- Default credentials (7 seed users)
- Project structure (27 model structs, 5 service layers)
- Architecture diagram
- Feature inventory by phase (auth, health records, vitals, encounters, bookings, menu, CSV import, webhooks, reporting)
- Database schema (27 tables, 3 materialized views)
- API endpoints (64 total)
- Authentication model (session cookies + API tokens + HMAC)
- Security features (bcrypt, RBAC, data scoping, HMAC, rate limiting, CSRF, file validation, audit trails, AES-256-GCM encryption, PII masking)
- Configuration (all env vars with defaults)

### 21. **static/js/app.js** (635 lines)
Client-side JavaScript utilities:
- **HTML escaping** - prevents XSS
- **CSRF injection** - auto-injects token into POST forms
- **API credentials** - obtains token from `/api/tokens`, caches in sessionStorage
- **HMAC signing** - client-side signing of requests (SHA-256 HMAC of `method:path:timestamp:bodyHash`)
- **apiFetch()** - wrapper that auto-signs all /api/* calls
- **File upload validation** - 10MB limit, MIME type check, success/error feedback
- **Draft save/restore** - sessionStorage-based with form IDs
- **Booking calendar** - renders slots from server response, handles slot selection
- **Partner matching** - fetches matches via API, renders partner list
- **Conflict check** - async check before booking submit
- **Date formatting** - MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM AM/PM
- **Modals** - audit trail, history, item settings
- **Menu management** - dynamic sell window rows, item settings form routing
- **Order management** - add/remove items, recalculate prices with member discount, order summary

---

## SUMMARY

This is a **production-quality full-stack application** with:
- **27 database tables** with proper indexing, partitioning (vitals), and materialized views
- **Multi-tenant org scoping** throughout (DataScope middleware, org-scoped queries)
- **5-tier RBAC** (student, faculty, clinician, staff, admin) with department-level access control
- **Dual authentication**: session cookies (browser) + API tokens + HMAC signing (REST)
- **AES-256-GCM encryption** for sensitive fields (SSN, health records)
- **Immutable audit logging** with SHA-256 fingerprints
- **Complex business logic**: booking state machines, conflict detection, dynamic pricing, time-based availability
- **Background workers**: CSV polling, temp access reversion, materialized view refresh
- **Comprehensive testing**: integration tests, unit tests for services
- **Security hardening**: CSRF protection, file magic byte validation, rate limiting, input validation, PII masking

All 20 files have been provided in full with complete implementations.

### 14. **internal/services/booking_test.go** (185 lines)
Tests for booking transitions, invalid transitions, 2-hour cancellation rule, conflict detection (requester/venue), virtual venue overlaps, slot generation, audit trails, partner matching.

### 15. **internal/services/menu_test.go** (218 lines)
Tests for pricing (dine-in/takeout/member discounts/promotions), sell window availability, holiday blackout, sold-out rejection, substitutes, order creation.

### 16. **cmd/server/main.go** (212 lines)
- Loads config, initializes encryption
- Connects to Postgres with retry loop
- Creates all services (auth, audit, webhook, health, booking, menu, reporting)
- Sets up handlers
- Starts background workers:
  - CSV watcher (10s polling)
  - Temp access revert ticker (1 min)
  - Materialized view refresh (15 min)
- Configures Gin router with 27 endpoints across:
  - Public: /login
  - Authenticated: /dashboard, /bookings, /menu, /health/*
  - Clinician: /clinician/*
  - Staff: /menu/manage/*
  - Admin: /admin/*
  - Internal API: /api/internal/* (HMAC + admin RBAC)
- Global middleware: rate limit, slow query logger

### 17. **internal/config/config.go** (92 lines)
- Config struct with DB, server, auth, file, rate limit, cache settings
- `Load()` - reads env vars with sensible defaults
- `requireEnvOrGenerate()` - generates random secrets if not set (with warnings)
- All settings: DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DB_NAME, SERVER_PORT, SESSION_KEY, HMAC_SECRET, FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY, UPLOAD_DIR, WATCHED_DIR, SECURE_COOKIES, GIN_MODE, etc.

### 18. **internal/auth/auth.go** (279 lines)
- `AuthService` with DB
- `Register()` - bcrypt hashing, creates user
- `Login()` - validates credentials, checks temp access, creates session
- `CreateSession()` - 32 random bytes → 64-char hex, 24h expiry
- `ValidateSession()` - checks expiry, active status, applies temp access
- `Logout()`, `InvalidateUserSessions()`
- `GrantTempAccess()` - elevated role with duration, auto-reverts
- `RevertExpiredAccess()` - background task clears expired temp access
- `SetUserSSN()` - encrypts SSN with AES-256-GCM
- `IssueAPIToken()` - creates locally-issued Bearer token (32 random bytes), stores expiry
- `ValidateAPIToken()` - validates token string, checks user active
- `ResetPassword()`, `RevokeAPITokens()`, `ChangeUserRole()`, `ToggleUserActive()`

### 19. **internal/services/crypto.go** (90 lines)
- `InitEncryption()` - loads FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var (base64, 32 bytes)
- `EncryptField()` - AES-256-GCM with random nonce, returns base64
- `DecryptField()` - decodes base64, extracts nonce, decrypts

### 20. **README.md** (545 lines)
Complete documentation covering:
- Overview (health clinic, athletics, dining, admin)
- Tech stack (Go, Gin, PostgreSQL, Docker)
- Quick start: `docker compose up --build`
- Default credentials (7 seed users)
- Project structure (27 model structs, 5 service layers)
- Architecture diagram
- Feature inventory by phase (auth, health records, vitals, encounters, bookings, menu, CSV import, webhooks, reporting)
- Database schema (27 tables, 3 materialized views)
- API endpoints (64 total)
- Authentication model (session cookies + API tokens + HMAC)
- Security features (bcrypt, RBAC, data scoping, HMAC, rate limiting, CSRF, file validation, audit trails, AES-256-GCM encryption, PII masking)
- Configuration (all env vars with defaults)

### 21. **static/js/app.js** (635 lines)
Client-side JavaScript utilities:
- **HTML escaping** - prevents XSS
- **CSRF injection** - auto-injects token into POST forms
- **API credentials** - obtains token from `/api/tokens`, caches in sessionStorage
- **HMAC signing** - client-side signing of requests (SHA-256 HMAC of `method:path:timestamp:bodyHash`)
- **apiFetch()** - wrapper that auto-signs all /api/* calls
- **File upload validation** - 10MB limit, MIME type check, success/error feedback
- **Draft save/restore** - sessionStorage-based with form IDs
- **Booking calendar** - renders slots from server response, handles slot selection
- **Partner matching** - fetches matches via API, renders partner list
- **Conflict check** - async check before booking submit
- **Date formatting** - MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM AM/PM
- **Modals** - audit trail, history, item settings
- **Menu management** - dynamic sell window rows, item settings form routing
- **Order management** - add/remove items, recalculate prices with member discount, order summary

---

## SUMMARY

This is a **production-quality full-stack application** with:
- **27 database tables** with proper indexing, partitioning (vitals), and materialized views
- **Multi-tenant org scoping** throughout (DataScope middleware, org-scoped queries)
- **5-tier RBAC** (student, faculty, clinician, staff, admin) with department-level access control
- **Dual authentication**: session cookies (browser) + API tokens + HMAC signing (REST)
- **AES-256-GCM encryption** for sensitive fields (SSN, health records)
- **Immutable audit logging** with SHA-256 fingerprints
- **Complex business logic**: booking state machines, conflict detection, dynamic pricing, time-based availability
- **Background workers**: CSV polling, temp access reversion, materialized view refresh
- **Comprehensive testing**: integration tests, unit tests for services
- **Security hardening**: CSRF protection, file magic byte validation, rate limiting, input validation, PII masking

All 20 files have been provided in full with complete implementations.

57	func (h *AdminHandler) RegisterWebhook(c *gin.Context) {
58		rawURL := c.PostForm("url")
59		eventType := c.PostForm("event_type")
60		secret := c.PostForm("secret")
61	
62		if rawURL == "" || eventType == "" || secret == "" {
63			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "url, event_type, and secret are all required"})
64			return
65		}
66		parsed, err := neturl.Parse(rawURL)
67		if err != nil || (parsed.Scheme != "http" && parsed.Scheme != "https") || parsed.Host == "" {
68			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid URL: must be http:// or https:// with a host"})
69			return
70		}
71	
72		orgID := getCallerOrgID(c)
73		h.WebhookSvc.RegisterEndpointForOrg(rawURL, eventType, secret, orgID)
74		c.Redirect(http.StatusFound, "/admin/webhooks")
75	}

3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"log"
6		"time"
7	
8		"campus-portal/internal/config"
9	
10		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
11		"gorm.io/driver/postgres"
12		"gorm.io/gorm"
13		"gorm.io/gorm/logger"
14	)
15	
16	func InitDB(cfg *config.Config) *gorm.DB {
17		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
18			"host=%s port=%s user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=%s",
19			cfg.DBHost, cfg.DBPort, cfg.DBUser, cfg.DBPassword, cfg.DBName, cfg.DBSSLMode,
20		)
21	
22		var db *gorm.DB
23		var err error
24	
25		// Retry loop — wait for Postgres to become ready (critical for Docker Compose)
26		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
27			db, err = gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{
28				Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Warn),
29			})
30			if err == nil {
31				sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
32				if pingErr := sqlDB.Ping(); pingErr == nil {
33					break
34				}
35			}
36			log.Printf("Waiting for database to be ready... (%d/30)", i+1)
37			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
38		}
39		if err != nil {
40			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database after retries: %v", err)
41		}
42	
43		sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
44		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(25)
45		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(10)
46		sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)
47	
48		return db
49	}
50	
51	func AutoMigrate(db *gorm.DB) {
52		err := db.AutoMigrate(
53			&Organization{},
54			&DepartmentRecord{},
55			&User{},
56			&TempAccess{},
57			&Session{},
58			&HealthRecord{},
59			&Vital{},
60			&Encounter{},
61			&Attachment{},
62			&AuditLog{},
63			&Venue{},
64			&TrainerProfile{},
65			&Booking{},
66			&BookingAudit{},
67			&MenuCategory{},
68			&MenuItem{},
69			&MenuItemChoice{},
70			&ItemSubstitute{},
71			&SellWindow{},
72			&HolidayBlackout{},
73			&Promotion{},
74			&MenuOrder{},
75			&MenuOrderItem{},
76			&APIToken{},
77			&WebhookEndpoint{},
78			&WebhookDelivery{},
79			&SlowQueryLog{},
80		)
81		if err != nil {
82			log.Fatalf("AutoMigrate failed: %v", err)
83		}
84	
85		// Create vitals partitioning by month.
86		// GORM creates a regular vitals table; we convert it to partitioned if it isn't already.
87		db.Exec(`
88			DO $$
89			DECLARE
90				month_start DATE;
91				month_end DATE;
92				part_name TEXT;
93				is_partitioned BOOLEAN;
94			BEGIN
95				-- Check if the vitals table is already partitioned
96				SELECT EXISTS(
97					SELECT 1 FROM pg_partitioned_table
98					WHERE partrelid = 'vitals'::regclass
99				) INTO is_partitioned;
100	
101				IF NOT is_partitioned THEN
102					-- Backup, drop, and recreate as partitioned
103					CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vitals_backup AS SELECT * FROM vitals;
104					DROP TABLE IF EXISTS vitals CASCADE;
105					CREATE TABLE vitals (
106						id SERIAL,
107						user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
108						weight_lb NUMERIC(6,1),
109						bp_systolic INTEGER,
110						bp_diastolic INTEGER,
111						temperature_f NUMERIC(5,1),
112						heart_rate INTEGER,
113						recorded_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
114						recorded_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
115						created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
116						PRIMARY KEY (id, recorded_at)
117					) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at);
118	
119					-- Restore data if any existed
120					INSERT INTO vitals (user_id, weight_lb, bp_systolic, bp_diastolic,
121						temperature_f, heart_rate, recorded_by, recorded_at, created_at)
122					SELECT user_id, weight_lb, bp_systolic, bp_diastolic,
123						temperature_f, heart_rate, recorded_by, recorded_at, created_at
124					FROM vitals_backup
125					WHERE recorded_at IS NOT NULL;
126					DROP TABLE vitals_backup;
127				END IF;
128	
129				-- Rolling partition: create partitions from 2025 through 2 years in the future
130				FOR yr IN 2025..(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM NOW())::INT + 2) LOOP
131					FOR m IN 1..12 LOOP
132						month_start := make_date(yr, m, 1);
133						month_end := month_start + '1 month'::INTERVAL;
134						part_name := 'vitals_' || TO_CHAR(month_start, 'YYYY_MM');
135	
136						IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = part_name) THEN
137							EXECUTE format(
138								'CREATE TABLE %I PARTITION OF vitals FOR VALUES FROM (%L) TO (%L)',
139								part_name, month_start, month_end
140							);
141						END IF;
142					END LOOP;
143				END LOOP;
144	
145				-- Create indexes
146				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_recorded_at') THEN
147					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_recorded_at ON vitals (recorded_at);
148				END IF;
149				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_user_id') THEN
150					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_user_id ON vitals (user_id);
151				END IF;
152			END $$;
153		`)
154	
155		// Create materialized views for reporting
156		db.Exec(`
157			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_clinic_utilization AS
158			SELECT
159				date_trunc('day', e.encounter_date) AS day,
160				e.department,
161				COUNT(*) AS encounter_count
162			FROM encounters e
163			GROUP BY day, e.department
164			ORDER BY day DESC;
165		`)
166	
167		db.Exec(`
168			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_booking_fill_rates AS
169			SELECT
170				date_trunc('day', b.slot_start) AS day,
171				b.venue_id,
172				COUNT(*) AS total_bookings,
173				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'confirmed') AS confirmed,
174				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'canceled') AS canceled
175			FROM bookings b
176			GROUP BY day, b.venue_id
177			ORDER BY day DESC;
178		`)
179	
180		db.Exec(`
181			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW IF NOT EXISTS mv_menu_sell_through AS
182			SELECT
183				mi.sku,
184				mi.name,
185				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity), 0) AS total_sold,
186				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity * moi.unit_price), 0) AS total_revenue
187			FROM menu_items mi
188			LEFT JOIN menu_order_items moi ON moi.menu_item_id = mi.id
189			GROUP BY mi.id, mi.sku, mi.name
190			ORDER BY total_sold DESC;
191		`)
192	
193		// Create unique indexes on materialized views (required for REFRESH CONCURRENTLY)
194		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_clinic_util_idx ON mv_clinic_utilization (day, department)`)
195		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_booking_fill_idx ON mv_booking_fill_rates (day, venue_id)`)
196		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_menu_sell_idx ON mv_menu_sell_through (sku)`)
197	
198		// Create audit log trigger function
199		db.Exec(`
200			CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION create_unique_index_if_not_exists(idx_name text, tbl text, col text)
201			RETURNS void AS $$
202			BEGIN
203				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = idx_name) THEN
204					EXECUTE format('CREATE UNIQUE INDEX %I ON %I (%s)', idx_name, tbl, col);
205				END IF;
206			END $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
207		`)
208	
209		log.Println("Database migration completed successfully")
210	}

41		dsn := os.Getenv("TEST_DATABASE_URL")
42		if dsn == "" {
43			dsn = "host=localhost port=5432 user=campus_admin password=campus_secret dbname=campus_portal sslmode=disable"
44		}
45		db, err := gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Silent)})
46		if err != nil {
47			t.Skipf("Skipping integration test — Postgres not available: %v", err)
48		}
49		sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
50		if err := sqlDB.Ping(); err != nil {
51			t.Skipf("Skipping integration test — cannot ping Postgres: %v", err)
52		}
53		return db
54	}

56	func setupRouter(t *testing.T) (*gin.Engine, *gorm.DB) {
57		t.Helper()
58		db := setupIntegrationDB(t)
59		cfg := &config.Config{
60			HMACSecret:  "test-hmac",
61			UploadDir:   t.TempDir(),
62			MaxUploadMB: 10,
63		}
64	
65		authSvc := auth.NewAuthService(db)
66		auditSvc := services.NewAuditService(db)
67		healthSvc := services.NewHealthService(db, auditSvc, cfg.UploadDir)
68	
69		bookingSvc := services.NewBookingService(db, auditSvc, nil)
70	
71		authHandler := NewAuthHandler(authSvc, auditSvc, db, cfg)
72		healthHandler := NewHealthHandler(healthSvc, auditSvc)
73		bookingHandler := NewBookingHandler(bookingSvc, db)
74	
75		r := gin.New()
76		htmlTemplates := template.Must(
77			template.New("").Funcs(tmpl.FuncMap()).ParseGlob(
78				filepath.Join("..", "templates", "*.html"),
79			),
80		)
81		r.SetHTMLTemplate(htmlTemplates)
82	
83		// Public routes
84		r.GET("/login", authHandler.LoginPage)
85		r.POST("/login", authHandler.Login)
86	
87		// Authenticated routes — mirrors production middleware stack
88		authed := r.Group("/")
89		authed.Use(middleware.AuthRequired(authSvc))
90		authed.Use(middleware.DataScope())
91		authed.Use(middleware.CSRFProtect())
92		{
93			authed.GET("/dashboard", healthHandler.DashboardPage)
94			authed.GET("/logout", authHandler.Logout)
95	
96			// Self-upload: all roles except staff (mirrors production)
97			authed.POST("/health/upload", healthHandler.UploadAttachment)
98	
99			// Health record mutations: clinician/admin only (mirrors production)
100			healthMut := authed.Group("/health")
101			healthMut.Use(middleware.RequireRole(models.RoleClinician, models.RoleAdmin))
102			{
103				healthMut.POST("/update", healthHandler.UpdateHealthRecord)
104			}
105	
106			authed.GET("/bookings", bookingHandler.BookingPage)
107			authed.POST("/bookings", bookingHandler.CreateBooking)
108	
109			adminGroup := authed.Group("/admin")
110			adminGroup.Use(middleware.RequireRole(models.RoleAdmin))
111			{
112				adminGroup.GET("/users", authHandler.UsersPage)
113				adminGroup.POST("/users/:id/role", authHandler.ChangeRole)
114				adminGroup.POST("/users/:id/toggle", authHandler.ToggleUser)
115			}
116		}
117	
118		// API routes — token + HMAC auth
119		api := r.Group("/api")
120		api.Use(middleware.APITokenRequired(authSvc))
121		api.Use(middleware.HMACAuth("test-hmac"))
122		api.Use(middleware.DataScope())
123		{
124			api.GET("/slots", bookingHandler.GetSlots)
125		}
126	
127		// Token issuance (session-auth)
128		authed.POST("/api/tokens", authHandler.IssueToken)
129	
130		return r, db
131	}

133	// helper: get an API token for a user
134	func getTokenFor(t *testing.T, db *gorm.DB, username string) string {
135		t.Helper()
136		authSvc := auth.NewAuthService(db)
137		var user models.User
138		db.First(&user, "username = ?", username)
139		require.NotZero(t, user.ID)
140		_, tokenStr, err := authSvc.IssueAPIToken(user.ID, "test", 1*time.Hour)
141		require.NoError(t, err)
142		return tokenStr
143	}

145	// helper: create an API request with token + HMAC signature
146	func newSignedAPIRequest(t *testing.T, method, fullURL, token string) *http.Request {
147		t.Helper()
148		bodyBytes := []byte("")
149		bodyHash := sha256Hex(bodyBytes)
150		ts := time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339)
151		// HMAC signs over the path only (no query string), matching c.Request.URL.Path
152		parsed, _ := url.Parse(fullURL)
153		sig := hmacSign("test-hmac", method, parsed.Path, ts, bodyHash)
154		req, _ := http.NewRequest(method, fullURL, nil)
155		req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+token)
156		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Signature", sig)
157		req.Header.Set("X-HMAC-Timestamp", ts)
158		return req
159	}
160	
161	func sha256Hex(data []byte) string {
162		h := sha256.Sum256(data)
163		return hex.EncodeToString(h[:])
164	}
165	
166	func hmacSign(secret, method, path, ts, bodyHash string) string {
167		message := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s:%s:%s", method, path, ts, bodyHash)
168		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
169		mac.Write([]byte(message))
170		return hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
171	}

593

605	func TestStudentCanUploadToSelf(t *testing.T) {
606		r, db := setupRouter(t)

608		var student models.User

610	
611		// Create a valid PDF-like multipart upload targeting self
612		body := &strings.Builder{}
613		body.WriteString("--boundary\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"user_id\"\r\n\r\n" + strconv.FormatUint(uint64(student.ID), 10))
614		body.WriteString("\r\n--boundary\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; filename=\"doc.pdf\"\r\nContent-Type: application/pdf\r\n\r\n")
615		body.WriteString("%PDF-1.4 test content") // PDF magic bytes
616		body.WriteString("\r\n--boundary--\r\n")
617	
618		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
619		req, _ := http.NewRequest("POST", "/health/upload", strings.NewReader(body.String()))
620		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=boundary")
621		req.Header.Set("X-CSRF-Token", "test")
622		req.AddCookie(&http.Cookie{Name: "session_id", Value: sessionID})
623		req.AddCookie(&http.Cookie{Name: "csrf_token", Value: "test"})

628	}
